title: ALIAS Missionized Autonomy for Emergency Services
solicitation_objective: Deliver a third-party autonomy application that integrates with ALIAS/MATRIX for emergency-service missions such as wildfire reconnaissance, suppression support, cargo sling load, or medevac-style operations.
source_basis: DARPA ALIAS Missionized Autonomy for Emergency Services program page and FAQ, January 2026.
opening_angle: ALIAS/MATRIX mission-app assurance for emergency autonomy
delivery: Integrable software module plus evidence/review tooling that watches for unsafe or degraded emergency mission-app behavior and supports operator review via a lightweight web viewer.
short_pitch: RTVLAS adapted as a wildfire and emergency-response mission-app assurance layer for ALIAS/MATRIX-style autonomy, monitoring whether missionized autonomous behaviors remain safe, timely, and recoverable during fireline reconnaissance and response operations.
phase2_transition: Tie the assurance layer to ALIAS/MATRIX-compatible mission autonomy middleware, add operator review workflows, and demonstrate recovery-state transitions in realistic wildfire reconnaissance or emergency response scenarios.
